token.markdownDescription:
GitHub API auth token

Specifies the GitHub API with which to authenticate. To learn more see [GitHub tokens](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/managing-your-personal-access-tokens).

repo.markdownDescription:
Name of target repo

Specifies the name of the target repo (e.g., user/repo)

branch.markdownDescription:
Target branch into which to commit

Specifies the name of the target repo branch (Default: repo default branch)

path.markdownDescription:
Path of artifact within the target repo to update

Specifies the path within the target [repo](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/github/#githuboutputreponame) and [branch](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/github/#githuboutputbranchname) to update.

header.markdownDescription:
Header value to write to output

Specifies as header value to write to the target [path](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/github/#githuboutputfilename). This value can specify a header row when emitting a .csv/.tsv lookup file or a header comment value for a .yaml file.

message.markdownDescription:
Commit message for target file

Specifies a commit message for this update. (Default: auto-generated message)

pullRequestBody.markdownDescription:
Pull request message for target file

Specifies the body of the pull request used to commit and merge the target [file](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/github/#githuboutputfilename) to the destination [branch](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/github/#githuboutputbranchname). (Default: auto-generated message)

squashMerge.markdownDescription:
Use squash merge when merging pull requests

Specifies whether to use squash merge (true) or regular merge (false) when merging the pull request. Squash merge combines all commits into a single commit, resulting in a cleaner commit history. This is useful for automated commits that run frequently (e.g., GitOps policy updates). (Accepts boolean or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: false)

fields.markdownDescription:
List of TenXObject field names to encode

Specifies a list of TenXObject intrinsic/calculated/extracted field names to extract and write to the output.

filter.markdownDescription:
A JavaScript expression an TenXObject must evaluate as 'truthy' against to be written to this output

Specifies a JavaScript expression that an TenXObject must evaluate as truthy to write its instance/template field values to this output.  For example, to only emit [timestamped](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+timestamped) TenXObjects, specify: ``` yaml githubOutputFilter: this.timestamped ```

encodeType.markdownDescription:
The output format in which to encode TenXObject values. Possible values:[json, delimited]

Specifies how to encode [githubOutputFields](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/github/#githuboutputfields) values to this output. Possible values: - **json**: formats names and values for the current TenXObject as: {"field":"str","field2":1, "field3": true} - **delimited**: formats values for the current TenXObject as: "str",1,true    The 'githubOutputEncodeDelimiter' argument determines the separator to use. (Default: delimited)

encodedLinePrefix.markdownDescription:
The line prefix prepended to the encoded event if 'githubOutputEncodeType' is 'delimited'

Sets the char which is prepended to the emitted TenXObject field values when [githubOutputEncodeType](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/github/#githuboutputencodetype) is `delimited`. (Default: )

encodeDelimiter.markdownDescription:
The delimiter placed between key and values pairs if 'githubOutputEncodeType' is 'delimited'

Sets the char delimiter to separate TenXObject field values when [githubOutputEncodeType](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/github/#githuboutputencodetype) is 'delimited'. (Default:  )
